methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Emelody is a Growth-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jon Thomas that has raised $10M+. The valuation analysis draws on a broader Robotics & Manufacturing peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (277810.KQ), including comparable-stage operators such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and produces an implied valuation of $12M, derived by applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement to the $10M total raised proxy. The peer-median approach is not applicable: while 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, none meet narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparability. Relying on all-sector median would introduce misleading noise without sufficient microstructural alignment.

The triangulated analysis, supported by one applicable method, indicates a valuation range of $10M (low) to $14M (high) with a median of $12M. This range assumes the $10M raised represents fair prior valuation and that standard Growth-stage progression multiples apply. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly comparable peers at Emelody's stage and tier, limiting confidence in external calibration, and the reliance on total-raised as a proxy for prior valuation rather than documented transaction multiples.
